title: eriosema populifolium subsp. capensis
intro: Eriosema populifolium subsp. capensis, also known as the Cape eriosema or Cape horse gram, is a flowering plant species in the family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South Africa, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and cholesterol. Eriosema populifolium subsp. capensis can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and stems are often used to make a tea or a poultice to apply topically.
